summary:
After manual mixing for approximately 45-60 seconds, alginate impression materials become just elastic enough to be placed into the patient’s mouth; this period marks their optimal working duration until they begin setting rapidly due to chemical reactions within minutes thereafter.
Alginate sets through sequential phases starting immediately upon mixing, transitioning first towards an initial flexible state suitable for insertion then progressing swiftly toward full hardness shortly afterward making accurate impressions critical during that narrow timeframe between flexibility and stiffness onset. To summarize:

The ideal working time for alginate impression material begins "just after" it shows slight elasticity, allowing manipulation, lasting around one minute before rapid hardening commences soon afterwards. This short interval ensures proper fitting prior to irreversible set-up.
